title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
Things to Keep in Mind before Buying a Life Insurance Plan
Life insurance is integral to comprehensive financial planning. You need to make a well-informed decision whilst availing of a life insurance plan. It is a long-term commitment and one that will offer much-needed financial protection to your spouse and children. Here are a few aspects which will help you make the right decision regarding your life insurance plan:
Life insurance should align with your life stage and financial goals:The quantum of life insurance should be increased in proportion to the phase of life you are in. The financial goals change as you grow older, and the number of dependents and the quantum of life insurance are directly proportional.
Moreover, you may choose to secure your financial goals, such as children's education and retirement, at least partially through insurance. In such cases, you need to choose the right type of insurance plan.
For example, money-back policies which offer intermediate payouts can be aligned to a child’s higher education, and the endowment plan or ULIP can be aligned to your retirement plan. The payouts can be conveniently used for your key financial goals. Pro tip: You need to review your life insurance requirements and ensure that they align with your goals and plans. You can plan these goals with a combination of investments, including insurance, thus enabling you to achieve them seamlessly. A combination of term insurance and other types of life insurance policies can comprehensively build the required quantum of life cover.
Assessment using human life value calculator:You can use the human life calculator available on our website to evaluate the estimated requirement of life insurance cover. Life coverage chosen should be sufficient to ensure that your specific needs are met. Pro Tip:Provide your correct information in the calculator to estimate the right sum assured for the policy.Note: The human life calculator will factor in your age, annual income, life cover, spending pattern, savings pattern, and debt (long term and short term) and assess your estimated life cover requirement.
Claim settlement ratio:The claim settlement ratio is yet another important aspect to consider. The ratio measures the percentage of claims that the insurance company has settled against the total number of claims that were received by the insurer. For example, Bajaj Allianz Life has a claim settlement ratio of 99.02%~.
Pro Tip
Along with the claim settlement ratio, you should also look at other aspects like the solvency ratio, credit ratings,, the plan benefits, etc. while opting for your life insurance plan.
Choose a suitable tenure:Choose a tenure that aligns with your financial goal. Assess your goals and their horizon and then choose a term in the life insurance plan. Pro Tip:List your goals when buying a policy. Check when you would need the funds for fulfilling the chosen goal and then select the term of the policy.
Compare products and understand the offerings:Like we do with all other purchases, you must also compare products and offerings within the life insurance space. Understand the benefits of the plan, its terms and conditions and how it aligns with your goals. Pro Tip:As a general rule, look for wider coverage within the life insurance plans. Verify the charges and the benefits offered, and look for hidden charges in products. You should also consider reviewing the product reviews to ensure that you commit to a product offering decent service touchpoints. Getting convenient servicing is helpful, especially during the later stage of your policy.
Avail tax benefits:The premium paid towards the life insurance plan can be claimed for tax benefits u/s 80C of the Income Tax Act, 1961 subject to provisions stated therein. The maximum limit allowed under this section is Rs. 1.5 Lakh. The maturity benefit is considered to be tax-free subject to the fulfilment of the conditions mentioned under section 10(10D) of the Income Tax Act, 1961.Further, the entire death benefits received by the nominee in the unfortunate event of the death of the insured remain tax-free in the hands of the recipient. Pro Tip:Life insurance,  one of the products that still offers you EEE (exempt, exempt, exempt) tax benefits and hence may be considered while doing your financial planning.
These are only some considerations you should be aware of whilst committing to a life insurance policy. You may have to evaluate the plans intricately to ensure that you get wide coverage for the optimised premium over the long haul. RLife insurance is also a way to plan for your financial goals. A prudent mix of insurance and other investments will ensure that you have adequate life coverage and can manage your financial requirements seamlessly.
Disclaimer:
In this policy, the investment risk in investment portfolio is borne by the policyholder. Investment in ULIPs is subject to risks associated with the capital markets. The policy holder is solely responsible for his/her decisions while investing in ULIPs. The views stated in this article is not to be construed as investment advice, and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale.
~ Individual Claims Settlement Ratio for FY 2021-2022
Bajaj Allianz Life Insurance Company Ltd., Regd. office Address: Bajaj Allianz House, Airport Road, Yerawada, Pune - 411006, Reg. No.: 116, CIN: U66010PN2001PLC015959, call us on toll free No.: 1800 209 7272, Mail us: customercare@bajajallianz.co.in, Fax No: 02066026789
Tax benefits as per prevailing Income tax laws shall apply. Please check with your tax consultant for eligibility.
full_pdf_text:
Things to Keep in Mind before Buying a Life Insurance Plan Life insurance is integral to comprehensive financial planning. You need to make a well-informed decision whilst availing of a life insurance plan. It is a long-term commitment and one that will offer much-needed financial protection to your spouse and children. Here are a few aspects which will help you make the right decision regarding your life insurance plan:
1. Life insurance should align with your life stage and financial goals:
The quantum of life insurance should be increased in proportion to the phase of life you are in. The financial goals change as you grow older, and the number of dependents and the quantum of life insurance are directly proportional. Moreover, you may choose to secure your financial goals, such as children's education and retirement, at least partially through insurance. In such cases, you need to choose the right type of insurance plan. For example, money-back policies which offer intermediate payouts can be aligned to a child’s higher education, and the endowment plan or ULIP can be aligned to your retirement plan. The payouts can be conveniently used for your key financial goals. Pro tip: You need to review your life insurance requirements and ensure that they align with your goals and plans. You can plan these goals with a combination of investments, including insurance, thus enabling you to achieve them seamlessly. A combination of term insurance and other types of life insurance policies can comprehensively build the required quantum of life cover.
2. Assessment using human life value calculator:
You can use the human life calculator available on our website to evaluate the estimated requirement of life insurance cover. Life coverage chosen should be sufficient to ensure that your specific needs are met. Pro Tip: Provide your correct information in the calculator to estimate the right sum assured for the policy. Note: The human life calculator will factor in your age, annual income, life cover, spending pattern, savings pattern, and debt (long term and short term) and assess your estimated life cover requirement. 3. Claim settlement ratio:
The claim settlement ratio is yet another important aspect to consider. The ratio measures the percentage of claims that the insurance company has settled against the total number of claims
that were received by the insurer. For example, Bajaj Allianz Life has a claim settlement ratio of 99.02%~. Pro Tip Along with the claim settlement ratio, you should also look at other aspects like the solvency ratio, credit ratings,, the plan benefits, etc. while opting for your life insurance plan. 4. Choose a suitable tenure:
Choose a tenure that aligns with your financial goal. Assess your goals and their horizon and then choose a term in the life insurance plan. Pro Tip: List your goals when buying a policy. Check when you would need the funds for fulfilling the chosen goal and then select the term of the policy.
5. Compare products and understand the offerings:
Like we do with all other purchases, you must also compare products and offerings within the life insurance space. Understand the benefits of the plan, its terms and conditions and how it aligns with your goals. Pro Tip: As a general rule, look for wider coverage within the life insurance plans. Verify the charges and the benefits offered, and look for hidden charges in products. You should also consider reviewing the product reviews to ensure that you commit to a product offering decent service touchpoints. Getting convenient servicing is helpful, especially during the later stage of your policy.
6. Avail tax benefits:
The premium paid towards the life insurance plan can be claimed for tax benefits u/s 80C of the Income Tax Act, 1961 subject to provisions stated therein. The maximum limit allowed under this section is Rs. 1.5 Lakh. The maturity benefit is considered to be tax-free subject to the fulfilment of the conditions mentioned under section 10(10D) of the Income Tax Act, 1961. Further, the entire death benefits received by the nominee in the unfortunate event of the death of the insured remain tax-free in the hands of the recipient. Pro Tip: Life insurance, one of the products that still offers you EEE (exempt, exempt, exempt) tax benefits and hence may be considered while doing your financial planning. These are only some considerations you should be aware of whilst committing to a life insurance policy. You may have to evaluate the plans intricately to ensure that you get wide coverage for the optimized premium over the long haul. Life insurance is also a way to plan for your financial goals. A prudent mix of insurance and other investments will ensure that you have adequate life coverage and can manage your financial requirements seamlessly.
Disclaimer: In this policy, the investment risk in investment portfolio is borne by the policyholder. Investment in ULIPs is subject to risks associated with the capital markets. The policy holder is solely responsible for his/her decisions while investing in ULIPs. The views stated in this article is not to be construed as investment advice, and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale. ~ Individual Claims Settlement Ratio for FY 2021-2022 Bajaj Allianz Life Insurance Company Ltd., Regd. office Address: Bajaj Allianz House, Airport Road, Yerawada, Pune - 411006, Reg. No.: 116, CIN: U66010PN2001PLC015959, call us on toll free No.: 1800 209 7272, Mail us: customercare@bajajallianz.co.in, Fax No: 02066026789 Tax benefits as per prevailing Income tax laws shall apply. Please check with your tax consultant for eligibility.
